message.content:
You are an adversarial concurrency and resource-leak auditor. Audit /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay for CONCURRENCY, THREAD SAFETY, and RESOURCE MANAGEMENT issues.

Focus areas (pass-3 extra: concurrency-guard discrimination):
- Race conditions in checkpoint.py pool/lock management (_CONN_INIT_LOCKS, _POOLS_BY_CONN, _acquire_migration_lock)
- Are threading guards actually tested? Remove synchronization mentally — does the test STILL PASS? If yes, the guard is untested.
- Context managers / file handles / DB connections that may not be closed on exception paths
- asyncio event loop misuse (mixing sync/async, blocking calls in async context)
- Thread-local vs module-level state assumptions
- Memory leaks: unbounded caches, growing collections, circular references
- Signal handling and graceful shutdown paths
- LangGraph graph node re-entrancy

Read tests/test_persistence.py concurrency tests closely. Are the mocks realistic enough to catch real races?

Return findings as JSON with id (C-N), severity, file, line, title, description, poc, suggested_fix.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- review: Review a pull request

content.content:
1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Upper bound (milliseconds) on how long the cross-process advisory lock may be waited for
56	# during schema bootstrap. A stuck/crashed peer that still holds a live PG session on the lock
57	# must NOT be able to hang bootstrap forever (#129); lock_timeout aborts the wait. 0 disables
58	# the bound (block indefinitely, the pre-#129 behavior).
59	_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "30000")))
60	
61	# Brief meta-lock guarding the process-local registries below (atexit registration, the open-pool
62	# list, the per-conn lock map, the pool cache). It is held ONLY for in-memory check-and-set — it
63	# is NEVER held across blocking cross-process I/O (pool.connection(), the advisory-lock wait, or
64	# setup() DDL). That blocking work is serialized per conn_str instead (#129), so a stuck advisory
65	# lock on one database cannot block initialization of an unrelated one.
66	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
67	
68	# Per-conn_str bootstrap locks: only same-conn_str initializers serialize; different databases
69	# bootstrap concurrently. Created lazily under _init_lock.
70	_CONN_INIT_LOCKS: dict[str, threading.Lock] = {}
71	
72	# Live pools cached by conn_str so the factory reuses one self-healing pool (and its worker
73	# thread) per database rather than opening a fresh pool on every call (#144).
74	_POOLS_BY_CONN: dict[str, Any] = {}
75	
76	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
77	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
78	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
79	_atexit_registered = False
80	
81	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL) has already run in this
82	# process — bootstrap once per process per schema; the advisory lock still guards the
83	# first-time cross-process race.
84	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
85	
86	# Strips ``://user:password@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
87	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
88	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
89	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[^@\s]+@")
90	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=...`` / ``pgpassword=...``),
91	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
92	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
93	
94	
95	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
96	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
97	
98	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
99	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
100	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
101	    """
102	    try:
103	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
104	        host = u.hostname or ""
105	        port = u.port
106	        scheme = u.scheme
107	        path = u.path
108	    except ValueError:
109	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
110	    if not host:
111	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
112	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
113	        host = f"[{host}]"
114	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
115	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
116	
117	
118	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
119	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
120	
121	    Layers, in order of precision:
122	
123	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
124	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
125	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://user:pass@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
126	       *other* URI in the text;
127	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=``/``pgpassword=`` strip for the keyword/value form.
128	
129	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
130	    not pass as ``conn_str``; the primary connection string is always scrubbed precisely.
131	    """
132	    if conn_str:
133	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
134	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
135	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
136	
137	
138	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
139	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
140	
141	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
142	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
143	    """
144	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
145	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
146	
147	
148	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
149	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
150	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
151	    that can raise)."""
152	    try:
153	        pool.close()
154	    except Exception:
155	        pass
156	
157	
158	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
159	    for p in _OPEN_POOLS:
160	        _safe_close(p)
161	
162	
163	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
164	    global _atexit_registered
165	    with _init_lock:
166	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
167	        if not _atexit_registered:
168	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
169	            _atexit_registered = True
170	
171	
172	def _conn_init_lock(conn_str: str) -> threading.Lock:
173	    """Return the per-conn_str bootstrap lock, creating it under the brief meta-lock (#129)."""
174	    with _init_lock:
175	        lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS.get(conn_str)
176	        if lock is None:
177	            lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS[conn_str] = threading.Lock()
178	        return lock
179	
180	
181	def _cached_pool(conn_str: str) -> Any:
182	    with _init_lock:
183	        return _POOLS_BY_CONN.get(conn_str)
184	
185	
186	def _acquire_migration_lock(conn: Any) -> None:
187	    """Acquire the cross-process schema-migration advisory lock with a bounded wait (#129).
188	
189	    ``lock_timeout`` aborts the ``pg_advisory_lock`` wait if a peer holds it past the deadline,
190	    so a stuck/crashed initializer cannot hang bootstrap indefinitely; the resulting error
191	    propagates as a redacted ``RuntimeError`` like any other backend failure. set_config is used
192	    (not ``SET``) so the value can be safely parameterized.
193	    """
194	    if _MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS:
195	        conn.execute(
196	            "SELECT set_config('lock_timeout', %s, false)", (str(_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS),)
197	        )
198	    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
199	
200	
201	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
202	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
203	
204	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
205	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
206	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
207	    or the extra is not installed.
208	    """
209	    try:
210	        if use_memory:
211	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
212	
213	            return MemorySaver()
214	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
215	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
216	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
217	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
218	        raise RuntimeError(
219	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
220	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
221	        ) from exc
222	
223	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
224	
225	    # Fast path: reuse a live, self-healing pool for this conn_str rather than opening a new pool
226	    # (and its background worker thread) on every call (#144). The liveness check on checkout
227	    # replaces dead connections, so a cached pool stays valid across transient outages.
228	    cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)
229	    if cached is not None:
230	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
231	
232	    # Serialize bootstrap PER conn_str — NOT process-globally (#129). Unrelated databases
233	    # initialize concurrently, and a stuck advisory lock on one conn_str can never block init of
234	    # another. The brief meta-lock (_init_lock) is only taken inside the helpers, never held
235	    # across the blocking advisory-lock wait / DDL below.
236	    with _conn_init_lock(conn_str):
237	        cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)  # another thread may have built it while we waited
238	        if cached is not None:
239	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
240	
241	        failure: str | None = None
242	        pool: Any = None
243	        try:
244	            pool = ConnectionPool(
245	                conn_str,
246	                min_size=1,
247	                max_size=8,
248	                kwargs={"autocommit": True, "prepare_threshold": 0, "row_factory": dict_row},
249	                check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
250	                open=True,
251	            )
252	            _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
253	            # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
254	            # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
255	            # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
256	            with pool.connection() as conn:
257	                _acquire_migration_lock(conn)  # bounded wait so a stuck peer can't hang us (#129)
258	                try:
259	                    # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
260	                    # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
261	                    # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
262	                    PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
263	                finally:
264	                    # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
265	                    # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
266	                    try:
267	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
268	                    except Exception:
269	                        pass
270	            with _init_lock:
271	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
272	                _POOLS_BY_CONN[conn_str] = pool  # cache for reuse on later calls (#144)
273	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
274	        except Exception as exc:
275	            # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
276	            # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
277	            # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
278	            failure = (
279	                f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
280	                f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
281	                "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
282	            )
283	            # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
284	            # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
285	            if pool is not None:
286	                _safe_close(pool)
287	                with _init_lock:
288	                    if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
289	                        _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
290	        raise RuntimeError(failure)
291	
292	
293	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
294	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
295	
296	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
297	    otherwise Postgres.
298	    """
299	    if use_memory is None:
300	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
301	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)

366	    }

369	        def execute(self, sql, params=None):
370	            if "lock_timeout" in sql:
371	                state["lock_timeout_on"].append(params)
372	            if "pg_advisory_lock" in sql:
373	                state["lock_on"].append(id(self))

386	            class _Ctx:

388	                    return conn
389	
390	                def __exit__(self_, *a):
391	                    return False

407	        for k, v in attrs.items():
408	            setattr(m, k, v)

548	    # so we WIDEN the cache-empty→setup→cache-write window with a small sleep inside setup — this
549	    # makes the test discriminating: it fails ("setup ran 8 times") if the per-conn lock is removed.
550	    import sys

1	"""Analysis-graph assembly + run/resume — the goal-agnostic orchestration wiring.
2	
3	The concrete nodes and topology of a study's analysis graph are the study's (adapter's)
4	responsibility — they encode dataset specifics and so must not live in the engine (ADR-0002).
5	The engine provides the reusable, hardened mechanics around them:
6	
7	- compile a LangGraph ``StateGraph`` with the durable checkpointer attached (so a run can
8	  interrupt at a gate and resume later);
9	- bound non-converging gate-rejection loops with a recursion limit, so a run that never
10	  converges fails loud (``GraphRecursionError``) instead of looping unbounded;
11	- stamp ``run_id`` as the checkpointer ``thread_id`` and onto traces (cross-store
12	  correlation), inside a trace context;
13	- the gate interrupt/resume protocol with its correlation guard (see
14	  :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode`).
15	
16	LangGraph is an optional extra (``orchestration``, ADR-0006), imported lazily.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	from typing import Any, Callable
22	
23	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import bootstrap_tracing, run_trace_context
24	
25	# Upper bound on graph super-steps per run. Bounds a gate-rejection→re-analysis loop so a
26	# non-converging run terminates loudly instead of hanging.
27	RECURSION_LIMIT = 50
28	
29	
30	def _require_langgraph() -> Any:
31	    try:
32	        import langgraph  # noqa: F401
33	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
34	        raise RuntimeError(
35	            "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
36	        ) from exc
37	    return langgraph
38	
39	
40	def compile_graph(
41	    state_schema: Any,
42	    *,
43	    build: Callable[[Any], None],
44	    checkpointer: Any | None = None,
45	    requires_checkpointer: bool = False,
46	) -> Any:
47	    """Compile a study's analysis graph.
48	
49	    ``build(builder)`` is a study-supplied callback that adds the study's nodes, edges, and
50	    entry point to the LangGraph ``StateGraph`` builder; the engine owns construction and
51	    attaching the durable ``checkpointer``. Returns the compiled graph.
52	
53	    Set ``requires_checkpointer=True`` for any graph containing gate nodes: ``interrupt`` is a
54	    silent no-op without a checkpointer, so a gate graph compiled without one would never park
55	    for the operator. The engine fails loud rather than ship a silently-broken governance
56	    graph (audit #G3). The engine cannot introspect the study-supplied ``build`` callback for
57	    gate nodes, so this is the caller's responsibility: **any study wiring**
58	    :func:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode.make_gate_node` **must pass
59	    ``requires_checkpointer=True``** (see GOVERNANCE.md "Gate interrupt/resume protocol").
60	
61	    Provenance note (#111): a gate node records its decision into LangGraph ``gate_decisions``
62	    state, which is **not** the hash-chained, tamper-evident trail. To get GOVERNANCE §3
63	    integrity on the durable path, pass ``make_gate_node`` a ``recorder`` that writes each
64	    decision into a :class:`~assay_engine.provenance.ProvenanceTrail`; without it, the graph
65	    path's gate decisions live only as graph state.
66	    """
67	    if requires_checkpointer and checkpointer is None:
68	        raise RuntimeError(
69	            "compile_graph(requires_checkpointer=True): a gate-bearing graph needs a durable "
70	            "checkpointer — interrupt() cannot park the run for the operator without one"
71	        )
72	    _require_langgraph()
73	    from langgraph.graph import StateGraph
74	
75	    builder = StateGraph(state_schema)
76	    build(builder)
77	    if checkpointer is not None:
78	        return builder.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
79	    return builder.compile()
80	
81	
82	def _thread_config(run_id: str, recursion_limit: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
83	    # Resume/run are keyed ONLY by thread_id — the checkpointer loads the latest (interrupted)
84	    # state itself. We deliberately do NOT thread a checkpoint_id: passing one is the
85	    # time-travel/replay API, and Command(resume=...) WITH a checkpoint_id replays from that
86	    # historical checkpoint instead of resuming the interrupt (version-dependent; LangGraph
87	    # issue #7361) — which would re-execute already-completed gates and break governance order.
88	    return {
89	        "configurable": {"thread_id": run_id},
90	        "recursion_limit": recursion_limit,
91	    }
92	
93	
94	def run(
95	    graph: Any,
96	    state: Any,
97	    *,
98	    run_id: str,
99	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
100	    trace: bool = True,
101	) -> Any:
102	    """Invoke a compiled graph for ``run_id`` (the checkpointer thread id), within a trace
103	    context, bounded by ``recursion_limit``. The run parks at the first gate ``interrupt``;
104	    resume it with :func:`resume`.
105	
106	    Uses ``durability="sync"``: a governance run must have its parked-at-gate checkpoint written
107	    *before* the operator is notified, so a crash in the (otherwise async) write window cannot
108	    lose the interrupt. (The LangGraph default is ``"async"``.)
109	    """
110	    if trace:
111	        bootstrap_tracing()
112	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
113	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
114	        return graph.invoke(state, config=config, durability="sync")
115	
116	
117	def resume(
118	    graph: Any,
119	    *,
120	    run_id: str,
121	    gate_id: str,
122	    decision: str,
123	    rationale: str,
124	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
125	    trace: bool = True,
126	) -> Any:
127	    """Resume a run parked at a gate with the operator's decision.
128	
129	    ``gate_id`` is the gate the operator intended to decide (from the governance channel) and
130	    is REQUIRED — the gate node's correlation guard rejects it if it does not match the parked
131	    gate, so a stale/misrouted decision cannot resume the wrong gate. Must use the SAME
132	    checkpointer the run started with (``thread_id == run_id``); resume always continues the
133	    latest interrupted state for the thread (no checkpoint_id — see :func:`_thread_config`).
134	    """
135	    _require_langgraph()
136	    from langgraph.types import Command
137	
138	    if trace:
139	        bootstrap_tracing()
140	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
141	    resume_value = {"gate_id": gate_id, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
142	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
143	        return graph.invoke(Command(resume=resume_value), config=config, durability="sync")
144

1	"""Human-in-the-loop gate node — the interrupt/resume protocol (GOVERNANCE.md).
2	
3	:func:`make_gate_node` turns an engine :class:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gates.Gate` plus a
4	proposal builder into a LangGraph node that pauses the run at the gate (``interrupt``),
5	surfaces a proposal to the operator, and on resume validates and records the decision.
6	
7	The **gate-correlation guard** rejects a resumed decision aimed at a *different* gate than the
8	one currently parked — a stale or misrouted decision (e.g. delivered late through the
9	governance channel) must not be applied to whatever gate happens to be waiting. The guard's
10	expected gate is the gate the operator named in the resume value, compared against this node's
11	gate; auto-deriving it from the parked gate would make the guard always match and thus a no-op.
12	
13	Two further hardened behaviors, ported faithfully from the prior platform's gate node:
14	
15	- **Re-prompt on a bad resume (not raise).** ``interrupt()`` consumes and *persists* the
16	  resume value into the checkpoint *before* the node returns, so raising on a malformed or
17	  mis-correlated value would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever — the gate
18	  would be permanently bricked. Instead the node validates inside a loop and re-fires
19	  ``interrupt()`` (carrying the error) on a bad value, so the operator can re-submit and
20	  actually recover.
21	- **Terminal-decision idempotency.** An ``approved`` decision is terminal: re-entering an
22	  already-approved gate is a no-op (it does not re-prompt or re-record). ``rejected`` and
23	  ``deferred`` are deliberately NOT terminal, so a reject→revise→re-review loop re-fires the
24	  interrupt as intended.
25	
26	The node returns a partial state update appending one record to ``gate_decisions``; a study's
27	graph state should declare ``gate_decisions`` with a list/add reducer so records accumulate
28	across gates. The decision/recording validator (:func:`apply_gate_decision`) is pure and
29	tested offline; only the ``interrupt`` call requires LangGraph (the optional ``orchestration``
30	extra, imported lazily).
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
36	
37	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
38	
39	GateProposal = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], Mapping[str, Any]]
40	
41	_VALID_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved", "rejected", "deferred"})
42	
43	
44	def apply_gate_decision(gate: Gate, resumed: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
45	    """Validate a resumed operator decision and return the state update to record it.
46	
47	    Raises :class:`GateError` if the resume value is malformed, targets a different gate
48	    (correlation guard), or carries an unrecognized decision.
49	    """
50	    if not isinstance(resumed, Mapping):
51	        raise GateError(
52	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resume value must be a mapping, got {type(resumed).__name__}"
53	        )
54	    target = resumed.get("gate_id")
55	    if target != gate.name:
56	        raise GateError(
57	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resumed decision targets {target!r} — stale or misrouted; "
58	            "rejecting (gate-correlation guard)"
59	        )
60	    decision = str(resumed.get("decision", "")).strip().lower()
61	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
62	        raise GateError(
63	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: invalid decision {decision!r} "
64	            f"(expected one of {sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)})"
65	        )
66	    # A rejection/deferral with no rationale is governance-relevant; record a sentinel rather
67	    # than a blank (incl. whitespace-only) so the audit trail is never silently empty.
68	    rationale = str(resumed.get("rationale") or "").strip() or "No rationale provided."
69	    record = {"gate": gate.name, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
70	    return {"gate_decisions": [record]}
71	
72	
73	# Only an approval is terminal: a re-entered approved gate is a no-op, but a rejected/deferred
74	# gate must re-fire its interrupt so a revise→re-review loop can re-prompt the operator.
75	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved"})
76	
77	
78	def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
79	    """True if ``gate_name`` already holds a terminal (approved) decision in ``gate_decisions``."""
80	    for record in state.get("gate_decisions", []) or []:
81	        if record.get("gate") == gate_name and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS:
82	            return True
83	    return False
84	
85	
86	GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
87	
88	
89	def make_gate_node(
90	    gate: Gate,
91	    propose: GateProposal,
92	    *,
93	    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
94	) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
95	    """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
96	
97	    ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
98	    asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
99	    ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
100	
101	    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
102	    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
103	    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
104	    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
105	    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
106	    """
107	
108	    def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
109	        if already_decided(state, gate.name):
110	            return {}  # terminal decision exists — do not re-prompt or re-record
111	        try:
112	            from langgraph.types import interrupt
113	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
114	            raise RuntimeError(
115	                "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
116	            ) from exc
117	        proposal = dict(propose(state))
118	        current: dict[str, Any] = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal}
119	        # Validate inside a loop: on a bad/mis-correlated resume, re-fire interrupt (next
120	        # resume index) carrying the error rather than raising — a raise would strand the
121	        # bad value in the checkpoint and replay it forever, bricking the gate.
122	        while True:
123	            resumed = interrupt(current)
124	            try:
125	                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
126	            except GateError as exc:
127	                current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
128	                continue
129	            if recorder is not None:
130	                for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
131	                    recorder(rec)  # land the decision in the hash-chained provenance trail
132	            return update
133	
134	    return node
135

1	"""Local vector store seam (ADR-0003) — loopback-enforced Qdrant factory.
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform. The client is constructed only against a
4	loopback host; the guard fires *before* importing the (optional ``persistence`` extra)
5	``qdrant_client``, so a misconfigured remote host is rejected regardless of whether the
6	package is installed.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	import uuid
13	from typing import Any, Protocol, Sequence
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
16	
17	VECTOR_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_VECTOR_HOST", "localhost")
18	VECTOR_HTTP_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_VECTOR_HTTP_PORT", "6333"))
19	
20	# Stable namespace for mapping the engine's string ids onto Qdrant point UUIDs.
21	_POINT_NS = uuid.UUID("a55a9e00-0000-4000-8000-000000000001")
22	
23	
24	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
25	class VectorStore(Protocol):
26	    def upsert(self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]]) -> None: ...
27	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]: ...
28	
29	
30	def vector_store_url() -> str:
31	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")
32	    return f"http://{host}:{VECTOR_HTTP_PORT}"
33	
34	
35	def get_qdrant_client() -> Any:
36	    """Construct a loopback-only Qdrant client (lazy import of the optional extra)."""
37	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")  # before any import
38	    try:
39	        from qdrant_client import QdrantClient
40	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
41	        raise RuntimeError(
42	            "vector store requires the 'persistence' extra (qdrant-client) — not installed"
43	        ) from exc
44	    return QdrantClient(host=host, port=VECTOR_HTTP_PORT)
45	
46	
47	def _point_id(ref: str) -> str:
48	    """Deterministic Qdrant point UUID for an engine string id (Qdrant ids must be int/UUID)."""
49	    return str(uuid.uuid5(_POINT_NS, ref))
50	
51	
52	class QdrantVectorStore:
53	    """Reference :class:`VectorStore` over a loopback Qdrant collection (cosine).
54	
55	    The engine's ids are arbitrary strings; Qdrant point ids must be int/UUID, so each id is
56	    mapped to a deterministic UUID and the original id is stored in the point payload (and
57	    returned by ``query``). This is a goal-agnostic reference implementation; a study may
58	    supply its own ``VectorStore`` (collection naming, distance, sharding are study choices).
59	    """
60	
61	    def __init__(self, collection: str, dim: int, *, client: Any | None = None) -> None:
62	        self._collection = collection
63	        self._dim = dim
64	        # Track ownership: only close a client we created ourselves; an injected client is the
65	        # caller's to manage (#117).
66	        self._owns_client = client is None
67	        self._client = client if client is not None else get_qdrant_client()
68	
69	    def close(self) -> None:
70	        """Close the underlying client if this store created it (no-op for an injected one)."""
71	        if self._owns_client:
72	            close = getattr(self._client, "close", None)
73	            if callable(close):
74	                close()
75	
76	    def __enter__(self) -> "QdrantVectorStore":
77	        return self
78	
79	    def __exit__(self, *_exc: object) -> None:
80	        self.close()
81	
82	    def ensure_collection(self) -> None:
83	        from qdrant_client.models import Distance, VectorParams
84	
85	        if not self._client.collection_exists(self._collection):
86	            self._client.create_collection(
87	                self._collection,
88	                vectors_config=VectorParams(size=self._dim, distance=Distance.COSINE),
89	            )
90	
91	    def upsert(
92	        self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]], *, batch_size: int = 256
93	    ) -> None:
94	        from qdrant_client.models import PointStruct
95	
96	        if len(ids) != len(vectors):
97	            raise ValueError("ids and vectors must have equal length")
98	        if batch_size < 1:
99	            raise ValueError("batch_size must be >= 1")
100	        # Build each batch of PointStructs lazily, so only one batch is materialized in memory at
101	        # a time (bounding BOTH payload AND memory) rather than boxing the whole corpus up front
102	        # (#118). Qdrant guidance: don't upload point-by-point, but bound the batch.
103	        for start in range(0, len(ids), batch_size):
104	            batch = [
105	                PointStruct(id=_point_id(ids[j]), vector=list(vectors[j]), payload={"ref": ids[j]})
106	                for j in range(start, min(start + batch_size, len(ids)))
107	            ]
108	            self._client.upsert(self._collection, points=batch)
109	
110	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]:
111	        res = self._client.query_points(
112	            self._collection, query=list(vector), limit=k, with_payload=True
113	        )
114	        return [(p.payload["ref"], float(p.score)) for p in res.points]
115

1	"""Data versioning — content-addressed, local, deterministic (METHODOLOGY.md §7, ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every finding must cite the exact bytes it was computed from. This provides a dependency-free,
4	on-box content-addressing store: an artifact is hashed (SHA-256) and copied under a local
5	store keyed by that hash, so the same bytes always yield the same version id and any artifact
6	can be re-fetched by id. (The prior platform used DVC via an external tool; this keeps the
7	engine self-contained — an adapter may swap in DVC by implementing the same Protocol.)
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import hashlib
13	import os
14	import re
15	import shutil
16	import tempfile
17	from pathlib import Path
18	from typing import Protocol
19	
20	_CHUNK = 1 << 20  # 1 MiB
21	_SHA256_HEX = re.compile(r"[0-9a-f]{64}")
22	
23	
24	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
25	class DataVersioner(Protocol):
26	    def put(self, path: str) -> str:
27	        """Version the artifact at ``path``; return its content hash / version id."""
28	        ...
29	
30	    def fingerprint(self, path: str) -> str:
31	        """Return the content hash of ``path`` without storing it."""
32	        ...
33	
34	
35	def _hash_file(path: Path) -> str:
36	    h = hashlib.sha256()
37	    with path.open("rb") as f:
38	        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(_CHUNK), b""):
39	            h.update(chunk)
40	    return h.hexdigest()
41	
42	
43	class LocalDataVersioner:
44	    """Content-addressed local artifact store. Deterministic and fully offline.
45	
46	    ``store_dir`` defaults to ``ASSAY_DATA_STORE`` or ``./.assay-data``. Files are stored under
47	    ``<store>/<aa>/<full-hash>`` (sharded by the first two hex chars).
48	    """
49	
50	    def __init__(self, store_dir: str | None = None) -> None:
51	        root = store_dir or os.environ.get("ASSAY_DATA_STORE") or str(Path.cwd() / ".assay-data")
52	        self._root = Path(root)
53	
54	    def fingerprint(self, path: str) -> str:
55	        p = Path(path)
56	        if not p.is_file():
57	            raise FileNotFoundError(f"cannot version a non-file path: {path!r}")
58	        return _hash_file(p)
59	
60	    def put(self, path: str) -> str:
61	        """Store the artifact under its content hash; return the hash (version id).
62	
63	        Idempotent: storing the same bytes twice is a no-op returning the same id. Publish is
64	        atomic — content is copied to a *per-writer-unique* temp file (so two processes
65	        storing the same digest never clobber a shared temp) and then ``os.replace``-d into
66	        place, so a reader never observes a partial artifact. The store is content-addressed,
67	        so an interrupted/duplicate write is harmless.
68	        """
69	        digest = self.fingerprint(path)
70	        dest = self._root / digest[:2] / digest
71	        if dest.exists():
72	            return digest
73	        dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
74	        fd, tmp_name = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=str(dest.parent), prefix=".tmp-")
75	        os.close(fd)
76	        tmp = Path(tmp_name)
77	        try:
78	            shutil.copy2(path, tmp)
79	            os.replace(tmp, dest)  # atomic publish within the same filesystem
80	        finally:
81	            tmp.unlink(missing_ok=True)  # no-op once replaced; cleans up on copy failure
82	        return digest
83	
84	    def path_for(self, digest: str) -> Path:
85	        """Local path of a previously-stored artifact by its version id.
86	
87	        The version id MUST be a SHA-256 hex digest (the only form :meth:`put` ever returns).
88	        Validating it rejects path-traversal payloads (e.g. ``../../etc/passwd``) that would
89	        otherwise compose a path outside the store (#112).
90	        """
91	        if not _SHA256_HEX.fullmatch(digest):
92	            raise ValueError(
93	                f"invalid version id {digest!r} (expected a 64-char sha256 hex digest)"
94	            )
95	        return self._root / digest[:2] / digest
96

1	"""Self-hosted tracing — OpenTelemetry → a local collector (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's Phoenix/OTel bootstrap. Everything is
4	on-box: spans export over OTLP to a loopback-bound collector; nothing leaves the machine.
5	OpenTelemetry and the Phoenix collector are optional (the ``observability`` extra) and
6	imported lazily, so this module loads — and the engine runs untraced — with neither present.
7	
8	The reasoning seam already emits spans through the global OTel tracer provider; calling
9	:func:`bootstrap_tracing` once at startup wires that global provider at a local collector so
10	those spans land somewhere. Without the extra (or with ``ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING`` set),
11	tracing degrades to a silent no-op.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import atexit
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import signal
20	import threading
21	from contextlib import contextmanager
22	from typing import Any, Iterator, Mapping, Protocol
23	
24	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
25	
26	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.observability")
27	
28	# Local collector endpoint (loopback-enforced).
29	TRACING_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_HOST", "localhost")
30	TRACING_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PORT", "6006"))
31	PROJECT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACING_PROJECT", "assay")
32	
33	_provider: Any = None
34	# Guards the _provider check-then-set so concurrent bootstrap_tracing() calls don't race.
35	_provider_lock = threading.Lock()
36	
37	
38	def _disabled() -> bool:
39	    return os.environ.get("ASSAY_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
40	
41	
42	def tracing_base_url() -> str:
43	    require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
44	    return f"http://{TRACING_HOST}:{TRACING_PORT}"
45	
46	
47	def tracing_endpoint() -> str:
48	    """OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint at the local collector (loopback-enforced)."""
49	    return f"{tracing_base_url()}/v1/traces"
50	
51	
52	def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
53	    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
54	
55	    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
56	    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
57	    """
58	    global _provider
59	    if _disabled():
60	        return None
61	    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
62	    with _provider_lock:
63	        if _provider is not None:
64	            return _provider
65	        try:
66	            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
67	            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
68	            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
69	            from phoenix.otel import register
70	
71	            provider = register(
72	                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
73	                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
74	                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
75	                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
76	                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
77	                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context
78	            )
79	            _provider = provider
80	            _warn_if_not_global(provider)
81	            _instrument_langchain()
82	            _install_flush_on_exit(provider)
83	            return _provider
84	        except Exception:
85	            # Missing extra / collector down / registration race — run untraced.
86	            return None
87	
88	
89	def _warn_if_not_global(provider: Any) -> None:
90	    """If another provider already won the global slot, our manual spans would vanish (#O6)."""
91	    try:
92	        from opentelemetry import trace
93	
94	        if trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
95	            _log.warning(
96	                "assay tracing: another OpenTelemetry provider holds the global slot; "
97	                "manually-emitted spans may not reach the local collector"
98	            )
99	    except Exception:
100	        pass
101	
102	
103	def _instrument_langchain() -> None:
104	    """Capture LangChain auto-spans (the bulk reasoning tier uses LangChain) — best-effort (#O5)."""
105	    try:
106	        from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
107	
108	        LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(skip_dep_check=True)
109	    except Exception:
110	        pass
111	
112	
113	def _install_flush_on_exit(provider: Any) -> None:
114	    """Flush buffered spans on clean exit (atexit) and on SIGTERM (#O3).
115	
116	    atexit does not run on ``kill``/container-stop, so a main-thread SIGTERM handler also
117	    flushes+shuts down. Both are guarded — tracing teardown must never raise.
118	    """
119	    flush = getattr(provider, "force_flush", None)
120	    if not callable(flush):
121	        return
122	    atexit.register(lambda: _safe(flush))
123	    if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread():
124	        try:
125	            prior = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)
126	
127	            def _handler(signum: int, frame: Any) -> None:
128	                _safe(flush)
129	                _safe(getattr(provider, "shutdown", lambda: None))
130	                if callable(prior):
131	                    prior(signum, frame)
132	
133	            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
134	        except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - not on main thread / unsupported
135	            pass
136	
137	
138	def _safe(fn: Any) -> None:
139	    try:
140	        fn()
141	    except Exception:
142	        pass
143	
144	
145	@contextmanager
146	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
147	    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).
148	
149	    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
150	    """
151	    if run_id is None:
152	        yield
153	        return
154	    try:
155	        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
156	    except ImportError:
157	        yield
158	        return
159	    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
160	    try:
161	        yield
162	    finally:
163	        context.detach(token)
164	
165	
166	# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
167	# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
168	# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
169	_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"
170	
171	
172	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
173	class Tracer(Protocol):
174	    @contextmanager
175	    def span(
176	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
177	    ) -> Iterator[None]: ...
178	
179	
180	class OtelTracer:
181	    """A tracer that emits OpenTelemetry spans via the global provider (no-op if OTel absent).
182	
183	    Use this where a component wants explicit spans rather than relying on auto-instrumentation;
184	    it honors whatever provider :func:`bootstrap_tracing` registered. Each span is stamped with
185	    its OpenInference ``kind`` so Phoenix classifies it (default ``CHAIN``).
186	    """
187	
188	    @contextmanager
189	    def span(
190	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
191	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
192	        try:
193	            from opentelemetry import trace
194	        except ImportError:
195	            yield
196	            return
197	        tracer = trace.get_tracer("assay_engine")
198	        with tracer.start_as_current_span(name) as sp:
199	            sp.set_attribute(_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND, kind)
200	            for k, v in (attributes or {}).items():
201	                sp.set_attribute(k, v)
202	            yield
203	
204	
205	class UnconfiguredTracer:
206	    """Fail-loud placeholder for contexts that must not silently drop spans."""
207	
208	    @contextmanager
209	    def span(
210	        self, name: str, attributes: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None, *, kind: str = "CHAIN"
211	    ) -> Iterator[None]:
212	        raise NotImplementedError(
213	            "no tracer configured — call bootstrap_tracing() and use OtelTracer, or supply a "
214	            "test double"
215	        )
216	        yield  # pragma: no cover - keeps this a generator/contextmanager
217

1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        for k, v in dict(params).items():
75	            client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
76	        return run_id
77	
78	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
79	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
80	
81	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
82	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
83	
84	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
85	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
86	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
87	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)
88

1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11

13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.

21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the

109	def _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records: list[ClaimRecord]) -> str:
110	    """Engine-computed content hash of the EXACT claim set that will be scored (#137).
111	
112	    The source's self-reported ``claim_fingerprint()`` is an unverified self-report — it can say
113	    anything. The engine therefore computes its own type-faithful hash over the materialized
114	    records (in scored order) and records THAT as the authoritative fingerprint, so an auditor
115	    re-deriving the fingerprint from the recorded/scored claims gets the value the trail records.
116	    """
117	    return hash_value(
118	        [
119	            {
120	                "claim_id": c.claim_id,
121	                "subject": c.subject,
122	                "referents": list(c.referents),
123	                "assertion": c.assertion,
124	                "provenance": c.provenance,
125	            }
126	            for c in claim_records
127	        ]
128	    )

131	def _require_unique_ids(ids: list[str], what: str) -> None:
132	    """Raise FirewallViolation if ``ids`` contains a duplicate (#138).
133	
134	    Identity (claim↔hypothesis↔verdict) must be unique within a run: a repeated id would
135	    otherwise be counted once per occurrence, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing
136	    alignment_rate / verdict accounting.
137	    """
138	    seen: set[str] = set()
139	    dupes: list[str] = []
140	    for i in ids:
141	        if i in seen:
142	            dupes.append(i)
143	        seen.add(i)
144	    if dupes:
145	        raise FirewallViolation(
146	            f"duplicate {what} within one run: {sorted(set(dupes))[:3]} — identity must be "
147	            "unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator / verdict accounting)"
148	        )
149	
150	
151	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
152	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
153	
154	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
155	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
156	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
157	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
158	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
159	    """
160	    return GateDecision(
161	        approved=True,
162	        gate=review.gate,
163	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
164	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
165	    )

170	    """Everything the runner needs for one run: the static definition + executable methodology.
171	
172	    ``definition`` is the adapter's binding (parser, modes, claims source, questions, optional
173	    feature builders). The callables below are the adapter's *executable* methodology, validated
174	    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;
175	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the
176	    definition).
177	    """
178	
179	    definition: StudyDefinition
180	    source: Path
181	    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder
182	    authority: TimestampAuthority
183	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit | None = None
184	    discover: DiscoverFn | None = None
185	    confirm_held_out: HeldOutConfirmer | None = None
186	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis] | None = None
187	    confirm_claim: ClaimConfirmer | None = None

189	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
190	        modes = self.definition.modes
191	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
192	            missing = [
193	                n
194	                for n, v in (
195	                    ("split", self.split),
196	                    ("discover", self.discover),
197	                    ("confirm_held_out", self.confirm_held_out),
198	                )
199	                if v is None
200	            ]
201	            if missing:
202	                raise ValueError(f"DISCOVERY mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
203	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
204	            missing = [
205	                n
206	                for n, v in (
207	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),
208	                    ("confirm_claim", self.confirm_claim),
209	                )
210	                if v is None
211	            ]
212	            if missing:
213	                raise ValueError(f"ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")

241	def _now() -> _dt.datetime:
242	    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
